perturbation.original_text: In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if, in the written opinion of such party's outside legal counsel, such disclosure is necessary in order to comply with, or avoid committing a violation of, any law or any rule of any applicable stock exchange or system.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if, in the written opinion of such party's outside legal counsel, such disclosure is necessary in order to comply with, or avoid committing a violation of, any law or any rule of any applicable stock exchange or system.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3, 'Evaluation Material' is changed to 'Confidential Information'. Later sections continue to refer to 'Evaluation Material' without defining it as the same as 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because Section 1 defines 'Evaluation Material,' and it's not clear if that definition also applies to 'Confidential Information,' leading to uncertainty about what is protected by the non-disclosure agreement. This is a clear example of misaligned terminology that could cause disputes over the scope of confidentiality obligations. The contradiction is taking place in Section 3, where its using the different term in contrast to the whole context of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE. In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if, in the written opinion of such party's outside legal counsel, such disclosure is necessary in order to comply with, or avoid committing a violation of, any law or any rule of any applicable stock exchange or system.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Evaluation Material,' which is defined in Section 1. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Confidential Information' in Section 3. The contradicted text also references 'Confidential Information', but because Section 3 was changed, there is now a misalignment. The 'Evaluation Material' is defined, but if section 3 uses 'Confidential Information' there is no clear indication of what that means, making the obligations for non-disclosure unclear, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The change creates a direct conflict and uncertainty within the document about which information is protected.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a party or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the other party's Evaluation Material, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall provide the other party with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a party or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the other party's Sensitive Data, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall provide the other party with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4, 'Evaluation Material' is replaced with 'Sensitive Data'. 'Sensitive Data' is undefined in the agreement, while 'Evaluation Material' is explicitly defined in Section 1. This substitution creates ambiguity as it is not clear if 'Sensitive Data' is intended to encompass the same information as 'Evaluation Material' or something different. This lack of clarity undermines the notification requirements and protective order process, leading to potential disputes over what information is subject to those procedures. The contradiction is appearing in Section 4, where its using the different term in contrast to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REQUIRED DISCLOSURE. In the event that a party or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the other party's Sensitive Data, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall provide the other party with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Evaluation Material' to 'Sensitive Data' in Section 4 introduces an in-text contradiction because 'Sensitive Data' is undefined, while 'Evaluation Material' has a specific definition elsewhere in the agreement. This creates ambiguity about what information is subject to the notification and protective order procedures, contradicting the requirement for clear disclosure obligations. The Contradicted Text has the term 'Sensitive Data' which is undefined, which now creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material shall be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, shall be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel shall be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Project Documents furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material shall be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, shall be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel shall be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, the phrase 'Evaluation Material' is changed to 'Project Documents' in the first sentence, while the rest of the paragraph continues to refer to 'Evaluation Material'. This creates a contradiction within the same section because the obligation to return 'Project Documents' is introduced, but the subsequent destruction or delivery obligations refer back to 'Evaluation Material.' Since 'Project Documents' is not defined, it's unclear whether the parties are obligated to return, destroy, or deliver all the same documents. This contradiction occurs specifically in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION OF DISCUSSIONS. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Project Documents furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material shall be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, shall be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel shall be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Evaluation Material' to 'Project Documents' in the first sentence of the changed text, while the rest of the paragraph refers to 'Evaluation Material,' creates a strong in-text contradiction. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text (which is the same text as the changed text). Since the definition of 'Project Documents' is not provided, it is unclear whether the parties must return, destroy, or deliver all the same documents. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which describes situations where multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definitions, leading to conflicting obligations. The contradictory nature is clear and direct, impacting the understanding and enforceability of the document.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Data Provided made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 6, 'Evaluation Material' is changed to 'Data Provided' in the first sentence, yet the rest of the paragraph refers back to 'Evaluation Material.' Because 'Data Provided' is undefined, the disclaimer of liability becomes unclear. It is uncertain whether the liability disclaimer applies only to 'Evaluation Material' or if it also encompasses 'Data Provided.' This inconsistency can cause disputes regarding liability for errors or omissions in the shared data. This contradiction is located within Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REPRESENTATION OF ACCURACY. Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Data Provided made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Evaluation Material' to 'Data Provided' in the first sentence of Section 6, while the rest of the paragraph refers back to 'Evaluation Material.' The 'Contradicted Text' is the exact same as the 'Changed Text' except for this modification. Since 'Data Provided' is undefined elsewhere, it is unclear whether the liability disclaimer applies only to 'Evaluation Material' or if it also encompasses 'Data Provided.' This ambiguity creates a strong contradiction, as it directly conflicts with the representation of accuracy and disclaimer of liability, making the disclaimer unclear.
